_doc: Excuses for Maya-only controls reported by generate_dcc_parity.py. DEFAULT IS GAP: a control absent here is an open, unreviewed gap. Add an entry ONLY when genuinely justified. Key = '<domain>:<objectName>'. Each entry: {"status": "na|divergent|done-elsewhere|planned", "reason": "<concrete why>", "label": "<human label, for readers>"}. status na = no Blender mechanism exists at all; divergent = a different Blender paradigm covers it (needs design); done-elsewhere = already built under another objectName/mechanism (say which); planned = accepted+scheduled (still counts as an open gap). A non-empty reason is mandatory; reasonless or bad-status entries are flagged and treated as gaps. Do not dump items here to make the number go down.

pivot:chk001.label: Reset Pivot Orientation
pivot:chk001.reason: Blender origins have no orientation independent of the object's rotation — there is no separate pivot orientation to reset.

pivot:chk005.label: Translate
pivot:chk005.reason: Transfer-translate is already what pivot tb002 (transfer_pivot) does; Blender's origin is translate-only so the per-channel checkbox is unconditional.

pivot:chk006.label: Rotate
pivot:chk006.reason: Blender's origin is a single point — there is no rotate pivot channel to transfer.

pivot:chk007.label: Scale
pivot:chk007.reason: Blender's origin is a single point — there is no scale pivot channel to transfer.

pivot:chk008.label: Bake
pivot:chk008.reason: Blender origins are always baked into the object; there is no separate bake-pivot step (already messaged on pivot's Bake Pivot button).

pivot:chk009.label: World Space
pivot:chk009.reason: Part of the World-Aligned manipulator-pivot option (tb003); Blender has no manipulator pivot distinct from the origin, so the world/local distinction does not apply.

pivot:chk010.label: Manip Pivot
pivot:chk010.reason: Blender has no manipulator pivot separate from the object origin — the transform gizmo pivots about the origin/median/cursor, not a stored manip pivot.

transform:chk038.label: Delete History
transform:chk038.reason: Blender has no construction/deformation history to delete (already messaged on edit's Delete History).

edit:chk026.label: Delete History
edit:chk026.reason: Blender has no construction history (already messaged on edit's Delete History button).

edit:chk027.label: Toggle Lock/UnLock
edit:chk027.reason: Node locking has no Blender analogue — datablocks aren't lockable the Maya way (already messaged on edit's Node Locking button).

edit:chk012.label: Holed (cleanup)
edit:chk012.reason: A Blender face is a simple polygon and cannot contain an interior hole, so there are no holed faces to detect.

edit:chk016.label: Shared UVs (cleanup)
edit:chk016.reason: Blender UVs are per-loop (face-corner), never shared across a vertex the Maya way — there is nothing to un-share.

edit:chk018.label: Lamina (cleanup)
edit:chk018.reason: A lamina face (two faces on the same vertex set) cannot exist in a bmesh — Blender rejects creating a duplicate-vertex-set face — so it can never occur (overlapping faces on DISTINCT vert sets are handled by chk025 / get_overlapping_faces).

edit:chk019.label: Delete Unused Nodes
edit:chk019.reason: Purging unused/orphan datablocks is the scene module's Cleanup (btk.cleanup_scene / scene b_cleanup); Blender has no construction-history node graph on this Delete-History op.

edit:chk020.label: Delete Deformers
edit:chk020.reason: Blender has no construction/deformation history to delete; deformers are non-destructive modifiers removed individually, not via a Delete-History op.

edit:chk030.label: Optimize Scene
edit:chk030.reason: Removing unused scene data is the scene module's Cleanup (orphan purge); there is no separate Optimize-Scene step in Blender.

edit:chk031.label: For All Objects (delete history)
edit:chk031.reason: Scope toggle for Maya's Delete-History op, which has no Blender analogue (no construction history to delete on all vs selected).

selection:chk009.label: Reverse Order
selection:chk009.reason: Blender operators receive an unordered object set; there is no object selection order to reverse (see the closed Reorder Selection / cmb001 N/A).

selection:chk003.label: Lock Values (island)
selection:chk003.reason: UI helper syncing Maya's per-axis Normal X/Y/Z island ranges; Blender's island-by-normal (tb002 chk_island_normal → select_linked delimit=NORMAL) uses a single normal-angle boundary, so there are no per-axis values to lock.

selection:s002.label: Normal X range (island)
selection:s002.reason: Maya grows the face island within a per-axis normal range; Blender's tb002 island uses select_linked delimiters (chk_island_normal stops growth at normal discontinuities by a single angle), not a per-XYZ normal range.

selection:s004.label: Normal Y range (island)
selection:s004.reason: Per-axis normal range for Maya's island growth; Blender's select_linked NORMAL delimiter has no per-XYZ range (see s002).

selection:s005.label: Normal Z range (island)
selection:s005.reason: Per-axis normal range for Maya's island growth; Blender's select_linked NORMAL delimiter has no per-XYZ range (see s002).

uv:uv_editor.label: Open UV Editor
uv:uv_editor.reason: Blender wires Open UV Editor on b031 (btk.open_editor('UV Editor')); the Maya header button name isn't reused.

uv:s014.label: Mutations
uv:s014.reason: bpy.ops.uv.pack_islands has no optimization-passes/mutations parameter — that is a RizomUV/u3dLayout-specific search knob with no Blender pack analogue.

uv:s011.label: Rotate Step
uv:s011.reason: pack_islands rotates by rotate_method (axis-aligned/cardinal/any), exposed as tb000 chk_pack_rotate — there is no min/max/step rotation search range (RizomUV-specific).

uv:s012.label: Rotate Min
uv:s012.reason: pack_islands has no rotation search range; rotation is the rotate/rotate_method toggle on tb000 (chk_pack_rotate).

uv:s013.label: Rotate Max
uv:s013.reason: pack_islands has no rotation search range; rotation is the rotate/rotate_method toggle on tb000 (chk_pack_rotate).

uv:chk016.label: Skip Instances
uv:chk016.reason: bpy.ops.uv.pack_islands has no instance-aware packing flag; the slot packs each selected mesh's UVs.

uv:cmb009.label: Pre-Scale Mode
uv:cmb009.reason: pack_islands exposes only a boolean `scale`, not Maya's Preserve-UV vs Preserve-3D pre-scale modes.

uv:chk022.label: Stack Similar
uv:chk022.reason: Blender stacks ALL targeted shells (b030 / stack_uv_shells); there is no per-shell similarity/tolerance grouping (Maya texStackShells-specific).

uv:s000.label: Tolerance
uv:s000.reason: Only drives Stack Similar (chk022) — Blender's shell stacking has no tolerance/similarity grouping to tune.

uv:cmb013.label: Non-Manifold handling
uv:cmb013.reason: Blender's bpy.ops.uv.unwrap doesn't reject non-manifold geometry, so Maya u3dUnfold's Warn+Select / Repair+Retry strategy has no analogue.

animation:chk020.label: Channel Box Only
animation:chk020.reason: Blender has no Channel Box — key ops act on all animated channels; there's no channel-box-scoped subset to restrict to.

animation:chk024.label: Channel Box Attrs Only
animation:chk024.reason: Blender has no Channel Box (no channel-box-scoped subset).

animation:chk033.label: Channel Box Only
animation:chk033.reason: Blender has no Channel Box (no channel-box-scoped subset).

animation:chk034.label: Channel Box Only
animation:chk034.reason: Blender has no Channel Box (no channel-box-scoped subset).

animation:chk_channel_box.label: Channel Box Attrs Only
animation:chk_channel_box.reason: Blender has no Channel Box (no channel-box-scoped subset).

animation:chk_override_layer.label: Use Override Layer
animation:chk_override_layer.reason: Blender has no animation layers (NLA differs structurally); bake targets the active action.

animation:chk_delete_inputs.label: Delete Inputs
animation:chk_delete_inputs.reason: Blender has no construction-history input nodes to delete after baking.

animation:chk_inherited_vis.label: Bake Inherited Visibility
animation:chk_inherited_vis.reason: Blender visibility (hide_viewport/hide_render) isn't inherited through the hierarchy the Maya way, so there's nothing to flatten on bake.

animation:chk001.label: Update
animation:chk001.reason: Blender's Go To Frame is a direct frame_set (tb000/s000) that always refreshes; Maya's auto-update playback toggle has no frame-nav analogue.

animation:chk010.label: Toggle Single frame
animation:chk010.reason: Maya Go-To-Frame single-frame playback toggle — not part of Blender's direct frame_set nav idiom.

animation:lbl020.label: Set To Current Frame
animation:lbl020.reason: Maya UI button to load the current time into the frame field; redundant in Blender where the timeline already exposes the live current frame.

animation:s001.label: Time (invert)
animation:s001.reason: Blender inverts keys in place; Maya's start-time placement of the inverted copy doesn't apply to an in-place mirror.

animation:chk002.label: Relative (invert)
animation:chk002.reason: Tied to Maya's inverted-copy time placement — N/A to Blender's in-place key mirror.

animation:chk005.label: Delete Original (invert)
animation:chk005.reason: Maya inverts to a copy then optionally deletes the original; Blender inverts the keys in place (no copy).

animation:chk003.label: Preserve Keys (adjust spacing)
animation:chk003.reason: Maya's Adjust Spacing can insert/preserve a key exactly at the shift frame via its keyframe-insert API; Blender's gap-insert is a pure shift of keyframe_points at/after the frame — nothing to insert-and-preserve.

animation:chk004.label: Relative (adjust spacing)
animation:chk004.reason: Blender's Adjust Spacing always shifts keys at/after the frame by a relative amount; there is no absolute-frame repositioning variant of a gap insert.

animation:chk006.label: Relative (transfer)
animation:chk006.reason: Blender Transfer assigns each target an independent copy of the source action (action.copy()); Maya's relative value-offset transfer (add the source delta to the target's current value) has no analogue in the action-copy model.

animation:chk007.label: Tangents (transfer)
animation:chk007.reason: Blender Transfer copies the whole action, so handles/tangents always come with it — there is no separate tangent-only toggle to expose.

animation:chk011.label: Invert (go to frame)
animation:chk011.reason: A Maya UI convenience that negates the frame spinbox; redundant in Blender where Go To Frame offers an explicit Absolute/Relative mode (cmb000) for negative/relative navigation.

animation:chk016.label: Group Overlapping (visibility)
animation:chk016.reason: Blender keys visibility per object at the chosen When frame (cmb002); Maya's combined-range group visibility keying is the stagger-style grouping machinery, kept to Stagger Keys (tb003 chk014).

animation:chk022.label: Untie Keyframes
animation:chk022.reason: Blender exposes untie as the chk_untie toggle on tb011 (tie_keyframes(untie=True)); the Maya objectName isn't reused.

animation:chk_absolute.label: Absolute Mode (scale)
animation:chk_absolute.reason: Blender Scale Keys uses a plain time multiplier; Maya's Absolute mode reinterprets the factor as a target duration/speed, part of its dual uniform/speed-retime model that Blender doesn't share.

animation:chk_ignore_holds.label: Ignore Holds
animation:chk_ignore_holds.reason: Blender's animation info reports each object's raw keyed extent (min/max); it does no static-hold segment analysis, so there are no leading/trailing holds to exclude.

animation:chk_merge_touching.label: Group Touching (scale)
animation:chk_merge_touching.reason: Tied to Maya Scale Keys' overlap-group machinery; Blender Scale Keys scales each action about its own pivot (no cross-object grouping). Stagger Keys carries the touching/overlap grouping (tb003 chk029).

animation:chk_mute_drivers.label: Mute Drivers
animation:chk_mute_drivers.reason: Maya mutes driver nodes after bake to keep them recoverable alongside its override-layer workflow; Blender's nla.bake replaces the action and Blender has no override animation layer, so there are no drivers left to mute.

animation:chk_preserve_outside.label: Preserve Outside Keys
animation:chk_preserve_outside.reason: bpy.ops.nla.bake replaces the action across the baked range with no preserve-outside-range option; preserving keys outside the range isn't exposed by the native bake.

animation:chk_split_static.label: Split Static Segments
animation:chk_split_static.reason: Maya Scale Keys can split animation into clips separated by static holds and scale each independently; Blender Scale Keys scales each action's keyframe_points as one block — the segment machinery isn't part of the Blender model.

animation:cmb001.label: Snap (go to frame)
animation:cmb001.reason: Maya snaps the resulting frame to 'clean' numbers (preferred/aggressive) via set_current_frame; Blender frames are integers and Go To Frame is a direct frame_set, so there's no fractional result to snap.

animation:cmb003.label: Rounding method (snap)
animation:cmb003.reason: Blender Snap Keys rounds to the nearest whole frame; Maya's floor/ceil/preferred/aggressive 'clean-number' rounding is its snap-mode machinery with no native Blender analogue.

animation:cmb004.label: Time range (delete keys)
animation:cmb004.reason: Blender Delete Keys clears all animation via animation_data_clear; windowed deletion (before/after/current) is done by selecting keys (tb013) and deleting them in the editor, not a delete-keys mode.

animation:cmb014.label: Scaling mode (uniform/speed)
animation:cmb014.reason: Speed modes retime by sampling world-space motion (units/frame) — a motion-analysis retimer specific to Maya ScaleKeys. Blender Scale Keys is a pure time multiplier.

animation:cmb033.label: Grouping (scale)
animation:cmb033.reason: Cross-object pivot grouping (single/per-object/overlap) is Maya ScaleKeys machinery; Blender Scale Keys scales each action about its own pivot.

animation:cmb034.label: Snap after scaling
animation:cmb034.reason: Snapping keys to whole frames after scaling is the Snap Keys op (tb009 / snap_keys); run it after Scale Keys rather than as a scale sub-option.

animation:cmb037.label: Which keys to step
animation:cmb037.reason: Blender Set Tangents applies the interpolation type to all keys of the selected objects; per-key scope (current-time/selected) is graph-editor key-selection nuance not surfaced on this op.

animation:cmb038.label: Copy mode
animation:cmb038.reason: Blender Copy Keys copies the active object's whole action (the paste buffer); Maya's per-attribute/per-frame/channel-box copy modes don't map to the action-copy model.

animation:cmb039.label: Paste mode
animation:cmb039.reason: Blender Paste links an independent copy of the action, preserving its own key timing (= Maya's 'at copy frame'); there is no value-at-playhead attribute paste in the action-copy model.

animation:cmb040.label: Tangent in/out (step)
animation:cmb040.reason: Blender interpolation is per-key (CONSTANT/LINEAR/BEZIER), not split into in/out tangent sides like Maya's stepped tangents, so there's no in/out/both choice.

animation:cmb_traversal.label: Dependency traversal (info)
animation:cmb_traversal.reason: Maya can expand the reported set by walking the dependency graph (up/downstream); Blender has no comparable per-object animation dependency graph to traverse.

animation:s007.label: Percent (intermediate keys)
animation:s007.reason: Blender adds intermediate keys by sampling on a frame step (every N frames); Maya's percent-of-key density is a different sampling model.

animation:s014.label: Samples (scale speed mode)
animation:s014.reason: Motion-sampling resolution for Maya's speed-retime mode only; Blender Scale Keys has no motion-sampling speed mode.

animation:b000.label: Slot Sequencer
animation:b000.reason: Maya's Shot Sequencer is a standalone shot-node / scene-per-shot ripple editor. Blender's sequencing model (the Video Sequencer, linked scenes, and timeline-marker cameras) is structurally different, so a faithful port is a separate Blender-native tool, not a parity control; the b000 slot points the user at the VSE + camera markers.

animation:b004.label: Shot Manifest
animation:b004.reason: Maya's Shot Manifest is a CSV-driven scene-assembly window built on Maya shot nodes. Blender assembles shots via linked Scenes/Collections (a different paradigm), so there is no faithful mirror; the b004 slot points the user at that native workflow.

scene:b006.label: Cleanup Unknown
scene:b006.reason: Maya removes 'unknown nodes' (a Maya plugin-loss artifact with no Blender analogue). The Blender cleanup equivalent - purging orphan datablocks - is the header's Scene Cleanup (b_cleanup / btk.cleanup_scene).

scene:b009.label: Fix OCIO
scene:b009.reason: Maya 'Fix OCIO' repairs a broken/missing OCIO config on scene load; Blender bundles its OCIO config (Filmic/AgX) natively and has no missing/broken-config failure mode to repair.

scene:b012.label: Toggle Command Ports
scene:b012.reason: Maya opens commandPort servers for remote control; Blender has no commandPort-server analogue (the same reason ScriptJobManager has no add_om_callback).

scene:b013.label: Mesh Converter
scene:b013.reason: Maya's standalone FBX->GLB Mesh Converter window; the Scene Exporter's 'Also Export GLB' (chk_glb, native glTF 2.0) covers GLB output for the live scene. Standalone batch-directory conversion is not part of the menu.

scene:b014.label: Save to Original Scene
scene:b014.reason: Maya saves an open autosave back to its resolved original file; Blender autosave recovery (File > Recover Auto Save) tracks no 'original scene' relationship - an autosave opens as a normal file you then Save As.

scene:lbl004.label: Open Workspace Root
scene:lbl004.reason: Blender has no project/workspace system, so there is no workspace root to open - a .blend is self-contained (recent-files list000 + autosave cmb002 cover quick re-open).

scene:lbl005.label: Auto Set Workspace
scene:lbl005.reason: Blender has no project/workspace system to auto-detect or set by walking up directories; a .blend is self-contained and derives its directory from the saved file.

rendering:t001.label: Scene-name regex
rendering:t001.reason: Maya's PlayblastExporter applies a regex to the scene name to build multi-variation output filenames; Blender's OpenGL render writes a single output path, set directly via t000 (Output base path). The regex batch-naming belongs to multi-file export, which the single render doesn't do.

rendering:chk056.label: Offscreen Capture
rendering:chk056.reason: Maya toggles offscreen playblast to dodge viewport-redraw artifacts; Blender's render.opengl always renders to an offscreen buffer (the 'Active Viewport' camera option in cmb041 chooses view_context), so there is no on/off equivalent.

rendering:chk057.label: Show Ornaments
rendering:chk057.reason: Maya burns viewport HUD/ornaments into the capture; Blender's OpenGL render shows overlays only when capturing the viewport (cmb041='Active Viewport' -> view_context), and HUD burn-in is the separate metadata-stamp system, not a capture toggle.

rendering:chk059.label: Clear Cache
rendering:chk059.reason: Maya clears temporary playblast files before export; Blender's OpenGL render writes straight to the output path with no playblast temp-file cache to clear.

transform:chk026.label: Make Live
transform:chk026.reason: Maya's Make Live turns a surface into a live construction grid (new geometry snaps onto it); Blender has no live-surface mode. Surface-snapping during transform is chk025 (Constrain: Surface -> FACE snap).

transform:chk040.label: From Channel Box
transform:chk040.reason: Maya freezes the channel-box-selected attributes; Blender has no channel box. The freeze targets are the explicit Translate/Rotate/Scale checkboxes (chk032-34).

transform:chk_restore_rig_anchors.label: Restore Rig Anchors
transform:chk_restore_rig_anchors.reason: Restores mayatk's GRP>LOC>GEO rig hierarchy (create_locator_at_object convention) after freeze; Blender rigging is Armature + vertex-group based with no such locator-anchor convention to restore.

transform:cmb_connection_strategy.label: Connection Strategy
transform:cmb_connection_strategy.reason: Maya DG: how to handle freezing T/R/S that are driven by node connections (preserve/disconnect/delete). Blender has no DG connection model - transform_apply bakes current values; driven channels are drivers/constraints, not disconnected or deleted by a freeze.

transform:s021.label: Snap Move Increment
transform:s021.reason: Numeric move-snap increment; Blender's increment snap uses the viewport grid, not a per-transform numeric value. The on/off is chk021 (Snap Move).

transform:s022.label: Snap Scale Increment
transform:s022.reason: Numeric scale-snap increment; Blender's increment snap is grid-driven with no per-transform numeric value. The on/off is chk022 (Snap Scale).

transform:s023.label: Snap Rotate Degrees
transform:s023.reason: Numeric rotation-snap angle; Blender's increment rotation snap is fixed/grid-driven with no exposed angle setting. The on/off is chk023 (Snap Rotate).

transform:chk023.label: Snap Rotate
transform:chk023.reason: Handled by the standalone chk023 slot (static widget in transform#submenu.ui, live toggle -> use_snap_rotate), not re-built in tb004_init like Maya - re-building would duplicate the chk023 objectName (its siblings chk021/chk022 are tb004 option-box-only). Same rotate-snap behavior, no objectName collision.

uv:cmb010.label: Pre-Rotate Mode
uv:cmb010.reason: Maya u3dLayout -preRotateMode is a 6-mode one-shot pre-orient using the 3D mesh orientation (incl. axis-to-V); Blender pack_islands rotation is the chk_pack_rotate boolean (rotate_method), not a 3D-orientation pre-orient.

uv:s004.label: UDIM tile
uv:s004.reason: Target UDIM tile 1001-1200; Blender pack_islands packs into the [0,1]/active tile, not a numbered UDIM - UDIM targeting is a post-pack tile offset, not a pack_islands parameter.

uv:cmb012.label: Scale Mode
uv:cmb012.reason: Maya polyAutoProjection -scaleMode (None/Uniform/Stretch); Blender smart_project exposes a scale_to_bounds boolean + correct_aspect, not the three-mode scale.

uv:chk000.label: Smart Fit
uv:chk000.reason: Maya smartFit best-fits the planar/cylindrical/spherical projection manipulator; Blender's Cube/Cylinder/Sphere projections (tb001 cmb011) auto-fit the object bounds with no smart-fit toggle.

uv:chk020.label: Straighten Shell
uv:chk020.reason: Whole-shell straighten; btk.straighten_uvs (tb005) is edge-based (selected UV edges within an angle band). Blender has no native straighten-entire-shell op.

uv:chk033.label: Per Shell (mirror)
uv:chk033.reason: Mirror each shell about its own center; btk mirror (transform_uvs / tb008) flips the whole map about the shared UV bbox - there is no per-island in-place flip.

uv:chk034.label: Preserve Footprint (mirror)
uv:chk034.reason: Keep mirrored UVs in the original footprint; Blender's whole-map mirror about the shared UV bbox center already preserves the footprint - the per-shell footprint option is specific to per-shell mirror (chk033).

uv:chk026.label: Include Auto Seams
uv:chk026.reason: Maya u3dAutoSeam auto-detects + cuts new seams without layout; Blender has no auto-seam-only op (smart_project generates seams as part of a full unwrap; existing island borders are chk025 Include UV Borders -> Seams From Islands).

uv:chk040.label: Invert Seam
uv:chk040.reason: Maya's unwrap_cylinder cuts an explicit lengthwise seam column and can flip it to the opposite side. Blender's Smart UV Project (the tb009 unwrap path) places the lengthwise cut automatically by connectivity/angle, with no exposed control over which side it lands on, so there is no Invert-Seam toggle to mirror.

polygons:chk008.label: U
polygons:chk008.reason: Maya divides a facet directionally (divisionsU). Blender's bpy.ops.mesh.subdivide is uniform (number_cuts in both parametric directions at once) with no per-face U-only / V-only operator; the Cuts spinbox (s009) is the Blender equivalent control.

polygons:chk009.label: V
polygons:chk009.reason: Maya divides a facet directionally (divisionsV). Blender's bpy.ops.mesh.subdivide is uniform with no per-face V-only operator; the Cuts spinbox (s009) is the Blender equivalent control (see polygons:chk008).

materials:b007.label: Hypershade Editor
materials:b007.reason: Blender's node-based Shader Editor is the Hypershade analogue; the header exposes it as b_shader_editor 'Shader Editor' (btk.open_editor). The Maya objectName isn't reused because a stateless button carries no shared QSettings state and the label differs (Hypershade vs Shader Editor).

materials:chk_exclude_defaults.label: Exclude Default Materials
materials:chk_exclude_defaults.reason: Blender has no auto-created built-in default materials (no lambert1/standardSurface1 equivalent — a new object starts with no material), so there is nothing to exclude from the report.

materials:chk_hide_defaults.label: Hide Default Materials
materials:chk_hide_defaults.reason: Blender has no auto-created built-in default materials to hide from the cmb002 material list (see materials:chk_exclude_defaults).

nurbs:s002.label: Degree
nurbs:s002.reason: NURBS surface degree. Blender's Revolve is a Screw MODIFIER producing a polygon mesh, not a parametric NURBS surface, so there is no surface degree to set.

nurbs:chk006.label: Range
nurbs:chk006.reason: Maya's 'force a curve range on the input curve' is a NURBS-curve parameterization flag; the Screw-mesh revolve has no curve-range concept.

nurbs:chk007.label: Polygon
nurbs:chk007.reason: Maya can output the revolve as a NURBS surface or a polygon; Blender's Screw modifier always produces a polygon mesh, so 'Polygon' is implicitly always on — there is nothing to toggle.

nurbs:chk009.label: Use Tolerance
nurbs:chk009.reason: NURBS build-to-tolerance vs section-count switch. The Screw mesh is driven purely by step count (Sections), so there is no tolerance build mode.

nurbs:s006.label: Tolerance
nurbs:s006.reason: NURBS surface build tolerance (used only when Use Tolerance is on). No analogue for the step-count-driven Screw mesh (see nurbs:chk009).

nurbs:chk000.label: Uniform
nurbs:chk000.reason: Uniform vs chord-length NURBS parameterization in the loft direction. btk.loft builds a polygon mesh by arc-length resampling (always uniform spacing), so there is no parameterization mode to pick.

nurbs:s000.label: Degree
nurbs:s000.reason: Loft NURBS surface degree. btk.loft produces a polygon mesh (linear bridge between profiles), not a parametric surface, so there is no degree.

nurbs:chk002.label: Auto Reverse
nurbs:chk002.reason: Maya auto-computes per-curve direction for the NURBS loft. btk.loft bridges the profiles in selection order using each profile's own point order; there is no NURBS direction-reversal solve to automate.

nurbs:chk003.label: Range
nurbs:chk003.reason: NURBS-curve range flag for the loft input (see nurbs:chk006); no analogue for the mesh loft.

nurbs:chk004.label: Polygon
nurbs:chk004.reason: Maya can output the loft as a NURBS surface or polygon; btk.loft always builds a polygon mesh, so 'Polygon' is implicitly always on.

nurbs:chk010.label: Angle Loft Between Two Curves
nurbs:chk010.reason: Maya's specialized two-curve angle-loft (extracts polygon edges as curves and lofts an angled surface between them) is a NURBS-surfacing feature with no Blender mesh-loft analogue.

nurbs:s007.label: Angle Loft: Spans
nurbs:s007.reason: Span count for Maya's two-curve angle loft (see nurbs:chk010) — no analogue in Blender.

rigging:chk001.label: IK
rigging:chk001.reason: Maya's tb000 radios pick whether the display-scale applies to joints / IK handles / IK-FK. Blender has no IK-handle display object (IK is a bone constraint, not a separate displayable handle with its own size), so there is no IK axis-display to scale. Bone local axes are toggled via tb000 chk000 (Joints -> armature.show_axes).

rigging:chk002.label: IK\FK
rigging:chk002.reason: No Blender analogue for a separate IK/FK display-scale (see rigging:chk001) — IK/FK is a constraint/driver setup, not a displayable handle.

rigging:s000.label: Tolerance
rigging:s000.reason: Maya's global joint/IK display-scale (jointDisplayScale). Blender has no global bone-axis display scale — bone display is per-armature display_type (octahedral/stick/...) and per-bone length, not a single scalar — so there is no equivalent value to set.
